perturbation.original_text: 6. REPRODUCTION AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall not be copied or reproduced without the Disclosing Party’s prior written permission, except for such copies as may reasonably be required for the purpose as set forth in Section I of this Agreem ent. Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient shall, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information, in whatever form, including written or electronically recorded information and al l copies thereof (other than copies retained in automatic back -up and archive systems), provided however that Recipient shall be entitled to retain one copy of the Confidential Information with its legal counsel or other appropriate corporate representativ e to evidence the exchange of information hereunder and in connection with legal or statutory requirements . All such retained copies shall remain subject to the use and disclosure restrictions in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. REPRODUCTION AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall not be copied or reproduced without the Disclosing Party’s prior written permission, except for such copies as may reasonably be required. Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient shall, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information, in whatever form, including written or electronically recorded information and al l copies thereof (other than copies retained in automatic back -up and archive systems).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'provided however that Recipient shall be entitled to retain one copy of the Confidential Information with its legal counsel or other appropriate corporate representativ e to evidence the exchange of information hereunder and in connection with legal or statutory requirements . All such retained copies shall remain subject to the use and disclosure restrictions in this Agreement,' the Recipient is now unconditionally obligated to return or destroy all copies of confidential information. This omission contradicts section 19 'SURVIVAL . Sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 17, 18, 19 and any sections (or parts thereof) which, by their nature, are intended to survive termination shall survive termination of this Agreement,' because without retaining a copy, survival becomes more difficult to evidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. SURVIVAL . Sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 17, 18, 19 and any sections (or parts thereof) which, by their nature, are intended to survive termination shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 omits the recipient's right to retain a copy of confidential information, contradicting Section 19, which states that Section 6 survives termination. The original text allows for retention to evidence exchange of information for legal reasons. By removing this, the changed text makes it more difficult to enforce the surviving clauses (including Section 6 itself) after termination, because there is no allowable retained copy. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because Section 6 surviving is impacted by the change.

perturbation.original_text: 15. NO WAIVER. Neither party waives any rights in invention or development lawfully possessed by it at the time of signing this Agreement. In addition, this Agreement does not imply any waiver of any rights or action under the patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret, unfair competition, fair trade or related laws. Failure to exercise any rights under this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. NO WAIVER. Neither party waives any rights in invention or development lawfully possessed by it at the time of signing this Agreement. Failure to exercise any rights under this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'In addition, this Agreement does not imply any waiver of any rights or action under the patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret, unfair competition, fair trade or related laws,' the agreement becomes ambiguous about the preservation of intellectual property rights, creating an in-text contradiction with section 10 'NO LICENSE. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidenti al Information is construed as granting either expressly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise, any license or right to the Confidential Information or any intellectual property rights embodied therein.' It is now unclear if the execution of the agreement affects any IP rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. NO LICENSE. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidenti al Information is construed as granting either expressly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise, any license or right to the Confidential Information or any intellectual property rights embodied therein.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement does not imply a waiver of intellectual property rights. The changed text omits this statement, creating ambiguity about whether the agreement affects intellectual property rights. This directly contradicts section 10, which states that the agreement does not grant any intellectual property rights. The omission in the changed text creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear if the execution of the agreement impacts IP rights, where the original text clarifies it doesn't imply any wavier of IP.

perturbation.original_text: 16. INUREMENT. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. INUREMENT. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing all references to 'Affiliates' and the definition of 'Affiliate,' the agreement becomes unclear about the extent of protection and liability for entities related to the parties. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the Recipient is no longer responsible for any breach from the Recipient's Affiliates. However, the first paragraph states 'This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, assigns and legal representatives.' This implies that the Recipient is responsible for breaches by any successors, assigns and legal representatives, but they are not identified by the same term 'Affiliates' thus creating an internal contadiction. This affects the enforcement of the NDA with related entities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. INUREMENT. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliates' and held the Recipient responsible for their breaches. The changed text removes the definition and any mention of 'Affiliates', while the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, states the agreement benefits successors, assigns, and legal representatives. By eliminating 'Affiliates' and their definition, the agreement becomes ambiguous regarding the Recipient's liability for related entities, creating an inconsistency with the benefit extended to successors, assigns, and legal representatives, leading to a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed in one section creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 21. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. No change or addition to any provision shall be binding unless it is in writing and signed by an authorized representative of both parties. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by electronic mail, registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) to the appropriate address set forth in the signature block below.
perturbation.changed_text: 21. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties. No change or addition to any provision shall be binding unless it is in writing and signed by an authorized representative of both parties. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by electronic mail, registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) to the appropriate address set forth in the signature block below.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto' from the entire agreement clause, the agreement might not cover prior oral agreements, and the scope of what constitutes the entire agreement is now less clear. This omission creates uncertainty that contradicts section 1's definition of 'Confidential Information', which details how that information is disclosed 'directly or indirectly, in writing g, orally or by inspection of premises or tangible objects.' If the entire agreement doesn't include communications related to confidential information or oral agreements, then disclosure by 'orally' is no longer protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The term “Confidential Information” as used herein means information that the Disclosing Party considers confidential. Confidential Information includes documents, drawings, models and software.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly limited the entire agreement to matters 'regarding the Confidential Information'. The changed text removes this limitation, broadening the scope of the entire agreement. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, section 1, defines 'Confidential Information' as including information disclosed orally. If the entire agreement, as defined in the changed text, does not cover communications related to confidential information or oral agreements as it did in the original text, then the disclosure of confidential information 'orally' as defined in Section 1 is no longer protected, hence there is a direct contradiction.
